report_name: DragonForce Ransomware Abuses Microsoft Teams TURN Servers for Covert Command and Control

threat_actors.name: DragonForce
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that compromised a major US services firm by hiding its command-and-control (C2) traffic within legitimate Microsoft Teams TURN server infrastructure.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The custom Go-based RAT abused Microsoft Teams' TURN (Traversal Using Relays around NAT) servers, which use web protocols, as a relay for its C2 traffic, camouflaging it as legitimate activity.

attack_patterns.name: Impair Defenses
attack_patterns.description: The attackers altered the `LimitBlankPasswordUse` security setting, allowing them to use accounts with blank passwords for remote access.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify System Firewall
attack_patterns.description: The attackers modified firewall rules to ensure their C2 traffic was not blocked.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Defense Evasion
attack_patterns.description: The attackers deployed an exploit for a then-undocumented vulnerability in a Huawei driver to mask their activity on the network.

attack_patterns.name: Create Account: Local Account
attack_patterns.description: The attackers created new local user accounts to maintain persistence within the compromised network.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The DragonForce ransomware was deployed to encrypt the victim's systems as the final stage of the attack.

malware.name: DragonForce Go RAT
malware.description: A custom Go-based Remote Access Trojan (RAT) designed to route its C2 traffic through Microsoft Teams' TURN servers to evade detection.

malware.name: DragonForce Ransomware
malware.description: The final ransomware payload deployed by the DragonForce group to encrypt the victim's systems after a prolonged dwell time.

tools.name: reg.exe
tools.description: Used by attackers to modify the 'LimitBlankPasswordUse' registry key to weaken security policy.

vulnerabilities.name: Undocumented Huawei driver vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: A then-undocumented vulnerability in a Huawei driver was exploited by the DragonForce group to help mask their activity on the network. The vulnerability was later disclosed by Huntress.

identities.name: Major US services firm
identities.description: A large US-based services company that was compromised by the DragonForce ransomware group.

infrastructure.name: Microsoft Teams TURN Servers
infrastructure.description: Legitimate Microsoft Teams TURN (Traversal Using Relays around NAT) relay servers abused by the DragonForce Go RAT to camouflage C2 communications.

indicators.name: Anomalous Microsoft Teams TURN Server Connection
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Microsoft Teams TURN Server Connection
id: 2b8c5a1f-3d4e-4b1a-9c8d-7e6f5a4b3c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects network connections to Microsoft Teams TURN servers originating from servers or non-interactive service accounts, which is anomalous behavior. Legitimate Teams traffic typically originates from user workstations.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/dragonforce-ransomware-hidden/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    DestinationHostname|endswith: '.turn.teams.microsoft.com'
    Initiated: 'true'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\teams.exe'
      - '\ms-teams.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects network connections to Microsoft Teams TURN servers originating from servers or non-interactive service accounts, which is anomalous behavior. Legitimate Teams traffic typically originates from user workstations.

indicators.name: Modification of LimitBlankPasswordUse Registry Key via CLI
indicators.pattern:
title: Modification of LimitBlankPasswordUse Registry Key via CLI
id: 8d9e0f1a-2b3c-4d5e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line modification of the 'LimitBlankPasswordUse' registry key, a technique used to weaken security policies for remote access.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/dragonforce-ransomware-hidden/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1562
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\reg.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'add'
      - 'LimitBlankPasswordUse'
      - 'HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line modification of the 'LimitBlankPasswordUse' registry key, a technique used to weaken security policies for remote access.

indicators.name: DragonForce Registry Modification for Defense Evasion
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: DragonForce Registry Modification for Defense Evasion
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-4a1b-8c2d-3e4f5a6b7c8d
status: experimental
description: Detects registry modification of the LimitBlankPasswordUse value, a technique used by DragonForce to weaken security policies.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/dragonforce-ransomware-hidden/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-16
tags:
  - attack.t1562
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: registry_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetObject|contains: '\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\LimitBlankPasswordUse'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects modification of the LimitBlankPasswordUse registry value, as seen in the DragonForce ransomware campaign.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Microsoft Teams TURN Server Connection
Modification of LimitBlankPasswordUse Registry Key via CLI
